common.metaDescription: Multi-platform freelancer career tool — enter your profile once, optimize it for every platform.

hero.title: Manage your freelance career <hl>from one platform</hl>.
hero.subtitle: Enter your profile once. AI adapts it for {platforms}, builds your CV and portfolio, and finds jobs that fit — all in one place.

showcase.title: Your whole freelance workflow, in one place
showcase.subtitle: Discover jobs, publish a portfolio, and export an ATS-ready CV — all from one profile.

headline.feed: Get a live job feed, scored to you
headline.portfolio: Publish a portfolio that wins clients

toolCta.unlockTitle: Your whole freelance workflow, in one place
toolCta.unlockDesc: Adapt your profile to every platform, discover matching jobs, and build a portfolio and CV — free to start, no card.
toolCta.shareText: I just used this free freelancer tool from Multifolio:

features.title: Everything in one place.
features.subtitle: All the tools you need as a freelancer, connected and automated.

adapt.title: Platform adaptation
adapt.desc: Enter your experience once. AI rewrites it into a tailored profile for {platforms} — each in that platform's voice.

portfolio.title: Portfolio site
portfolio.desc: Publish a polished portfolio page from your profile in seconds — shareable link, no code, no design work.

matching.title: Job matching
matching.desc: Paste a job post and get a 0–100 fit score with strengths, gaps and the requirements it extracted — so you only spend effort where you can win.

tracking.title: Application tracking
tracking.desc: Track applications on a drag-and-drop kanban board and see your conversion funnel — response, interview and offer rates at a glance.

ai.title: AI generation
ai.desc: Generate platform-specific proposals covering the post's requirements, then patch any gaps with one click.

cv.title: AI CV builder
cv.desc: Turn your profile into a clean, recruiter-ready CV with 8 ATS-ready templates. Reorder sections by drag-and-drop and export to a selectable-text PDF in one click.

discovery.title: Job discovery
discovery.desc: A live feed of remote jobs scored against your profile. Save searches, star roles, and get an in-app alert when a strong new match lands.

secure.title: Secure & fast
secure.desc: Your data is encrypted and only yours — no one else can reach your account. Security is on by default.

step1.title: Enter your profile once
step1.desc: Add your work, skills and results a single time — no per-platform copy-paste.

step2.title: AI adapts & builds
step2.desc: Get optimized text for all five platforms plus a ready-to-share portfolio and an exportable CV.

step3.title: Match, pitch & track
step3.desc: Score job posts, discover new ones, generate proposals that fit, and track every application in one place.

tools.title: Try it before you sign up
tools.subtitle: Instant, no account needed. Get a taste of what Multifolio does for your freelance career.

analyze.title: Profile check
analyze.desc: Paste a profile URL and get an instant 0–100 score with concrete fixes.

earnings.title: Earnings calculator
earnings.desc: See exactly what lands in your pocket after platform, payment and tax cuts.

rate.title: Rate calculator
rate.desc: Find the hourly rate you need to hit your income goal.

roi.title: Connects ROI calculator
roi.desc: See if the Connects you spend on proposals actually earn their money back.

proposalChecker.title: Proposal checker
proposalChecker.desc: Score your cover letter against what actually wins clients.

headlineOptimizer.title: Headline optimizer
headlineOptimizer.desc: Score your profile headline against what gets clients.

compare.title: Platform comparison
compare.desc: Compare fees, audience and payout speed across five platforms to pick where to focus.

grow.title: Set up in minutes, not hours

import.title: One-click import
import.desc: Bring your Upwork, Fiverr and LinkedIn profile in with our browser extension — or paste a URL, text or PDF. No copy-paste marathon.

referral.title: Invite & earn
referral.desc: Invite a friend and you both get +20 credits when they build their first profile. Everyone starts with 100 free.

pricing.title: Pay only for what you use
pricing.subtitle: Credit-based, pay-as-you-go. No subscription, no auto-renew traps. Start with 100 free credits.
pricing.paymentSoon: Credit purchasing is coming soon — for now everyone starts with 100 free credits.
pricing.tryApprox: TL prices are approximate USD equivalents; the exact amount is confirmed at checkout when purchasing opens.
pricing.antiConnects: Credits pay for work you keep — optimized profiles and proposals that stay yours. No subscription traps, no per-bid "Connects" fees, and no charge for jobs you don't win.

desc.starter: Great for trying things out — a month of active adapting, matching and proposals.
desc.pro: For active freelancers applying every week. Best value per credit.
desc.scale: For power users and small agencies running at volume.

q.beta: Is it really free to start?

a.credits: Credits are what you spend on AI actions. Adapting a profile or matching a job costs 2 credits, a proposal 3, a full portfolio or CV 5. You always see your balance before you spend.
a.expire: No. Credits never expire — buy once, use whenever. There's no monthly reset.
a.subscription: No subscription and no auto-renew. It's pay-as-you-go: top up credits only when you want to.
a.beta: Yes. Every new account starts with 100 free credits — enough to adapt all five platforms, build a portfolio and match several jobs.

finalCta.title: Take your freelance career to the next level.
finalCta.subtitle: Start free with 100 credits — no card required, pay only if you need more.

mockup.scoreNote: Strong profile. Headline optimization is the biggest opportunity.

demos.proposal: Hi! I read your post — I've shipped three similar React dashboards. Here's how I'd approach yours…

referral.shareText: I'm using Multifolio to win more freelance work across platforms — try it free:

weeklyDigest.label: Weekly summary email
weeklyDigest.hint: A weekly recap of your applications, proposals and new feed matches.

profileStrength.title: Setup progress
profileStrength.hint: Complete profiles get noticed far more often. Finish these steps:

items.headline: Write a stronger headline
items.adapted: Adapt your profile for a platform

pipeline.referralNote: {count, plural, one {# application came via referral} other {# applications came via referral}} — referred candidates convert far better.

cashflow.title: Cash-flow projection
cashflow.disclaimer: Rough estimate from budget text, weighted by stage. Mixed currencies not converted.

jobs.emptyHint: Start with "Add Job"; match with AI and track your applications.

followup.waiting: {days, plural, one {No reply for # day} other {No reply for # days}}
followup.hint: A short, polite follow-up can bring your application back to the top.
followup.hintSecond: It's been a while — one last, respectful nudge is worth a shot before you move on.
followup.error: Could not generate the follow-up message. Try again.

interview.hint: Get STAR stories from your own projects, a 'tell me about yourself' script, and questions to ask.

negotiation.banner: Got an offer? Don't accept it yet.
negotiation.hint: Get a fair-value read, a counter-offer range, talking points, and a ready-to-send email.

add.title: Add Job
add.referredLabel: I was referred to this role (internal referral)
add.matchError: Job added, but AI matching failed. You can match it manually from the job.

analytics.noUsageHint: It will appear here as you adapt, generate portfolios or match listings.
analytics.winRateHint: Do higher AI match scores actually win more? This tracks your outcomes by score band.

quality.label: Proposal quality
quality.allGood: Looks strong — clear, specific, and the right length.

issue.wordCountLow: Too short — add a bit more detail or proof.
issue.wordCountHigh: Too long — clients skim; tighten it up.
issue.noCta: No call-to-action — end with 'Let's discuss' or similar.
issue.generic: Generic opening — start with the client's problem, not your expertise.
issue.placeholder: Unedited placeholder left in — remove {…} / […] before sending.
issue.notSpecific: Too generic — mention a specific detail from the job post (a tool, skill, or requirement they named).

tip.title: What wins on most platforms
tip.hook: Open with the client's problem, not your resume.
tip.proof: Add one concrete proof — a result, metric, or relevant project.
tip.cta: End with a clear next step: "Let's discuss" or "When can we start?"

modal.title: Create Proposal
modal.translationNote: For your reference only — send the original text above to the platform.

profile.translateHint: Translate your profile to your language.
profile.headlinePlaceholder: e.g. Senior Frontend Developer · React & TypeScript
profile.summaryPlaceholder: Briefly describe your experience, expertise, and standout results.
profile.skillsHint: Press Enter or comma to add each skill.
profile.addPhotosDesc: Pick photos from your connected profiles
profile.identityUntitled: Add a headline to define your identity

health.title: Account safety check

items.email: Email address — marketplaces ban taking clients off-platform; sharing contact info risks account suspension.
items.phone: Phone number — same off-platform contact risk.
items.messaging: Messaging app (WhatsApp/Telegram/Skype) — off-platform contact is prohibited.
items.payment: Off-platform payment — bypassing platform fees is one of the most serious violations and a common ban reason.
items.external_link: External link — some platforms restrict links in profiles/proposals; review before sending.

health.footnote: This is a heads-up, not a block — you can still copy the text. Rules vary by platform.

platforms.hubSubtitle: Manage each platform's profile, connection, matched jobs and proposals in one place.

adaptAll.title: Optimize for every platform
adaptAll.desc: Adapt your profile to your chosen platforms in one click — each in its own voice.
adaptAll.selectHint: Pick the platforms to adapt ({count} selected):
adaptAll.done: Done — adapted for {count} platforms. Open any platform to see the result.
adaptAll.partial: Adapted {count} platforms — you ran out of credits for the rest.
adaptAll.error: Couldn't adapt your platforms. Please try again.

card.jobsCount: {count, plural, =0 {No matched jobs} one {# matched job} other {# matched jobs}}
card.saveProfileFirst: Save your profile first to adapt it and match jobs.

detail.profileHint: AI-optimized profile copy for this platform.
detail.sourceProfileHint: This data powers adaptation, job matching and proposals on every platform.
detail.translateSaveHint: Translate this profile and save it (Refresh re-fetches the original).
detail.syncEmptyHint: Pull your public {platform} profile (photo, bio, skills) from the connected URL.
detail.syncNoUrlHint: Save your profile URL in the Connection section first, then fetch your data.
detail.syncExtensionHint: {platform} blocks server access — open your profile page in the browser and import with the Multifolio extension. The data will appear here.
detail.notSupportedYet: Profile import for {platform} isn't available yet — your core profile is used instead. You can still adapt your profile and generate proposals for it.
detail.noJobs: No jobs for this platform yet. Add one from the Jobs page.
detail.regenerateWarn: Regenerating replaces the current text — copy it first if you want to keep it.
detail.noProposals: No proposals for this platform yet.

linkedin: Lead your headline with your role and a clear value proposition.
Write the About section in first person; back claims with concrete results.
Use industry keywords naturally so recruiters find you.
upwork: Open with the client's problem, then how you solve it.
Keep it scannable — short paragraphs and clear outcomes.
Name your specialty precisely (e.g. 'React & Next.js Frontend Developer').
fiverr: Frame your profile around a productized, repeatable service.
Highlight fast delivery and a clear scope of what's included.
Use benefit-driven language buyers can grasp in seconds.
freelancer: Win bids by showing you understood the project in the first two lines.
Highlight delivery reliability and concrete outcomes over generic claims.
Load your profile with the exact skill keywords clients search for.
contra: Lead with your personal brand — who you are and the results you deliver.
Let your portfolio do the talking; link real work as proof.
Keep the tone warm and independent — commission-free, quality-first.
peopleperhour: Target the client's problem directly in your opening lines.
Offer clear fixed-price 'Hourlies' for repeatable, well-scoped work.
Emphasize reliable delivery and sprinkle relevant skill keywords.
99designs: State your design specialty precisely (e.g. 'Logo & Brand Identity Designer').
Let visuals carry the pitch — reference a relevant piece from your portfolio.
Be creative but outcome-focused about the problem you solve.
guru: Show you grasp the project scope in the first two sentences of your quote.
Back your expertise with concrete, measurable results.
Be clear about process and delivery to build trust across categories.

portfolio.title: Portfolio Site
portfolio.description: Create a public page from your profile and share it.
portfolio.saveProfileFirst: Save your profile first to create a portfolio.
portfolio.displayHint: How your imported images appear on your public page.
portfolio.displayNoProjects: No projects to show by project yet. Import an Upwork/Fiverr profile, then save to sync them here — no regeneration needed.
portfolio.displaySyncPending: {count} imported projects ready. Click Save settings to publish them to your page.

leads.title: Incoming requests
leads.empty: No requests yet. Share your portfolio to start receiving them.

portfolio.galleryHint: Pulled from your connected profiles. Remove any you don't want shown.
portfolio.contactHint: Shown as a “Get in touch” button on your public page. Email is used first; leave both empty to hide it.
portfolio.contactEmailInvalid: Not a valid email — the button won't show until it's fixed.
portfolio.contactUrlInvalid: Enter a full URL starting with http:// or https:// — otherwise the button won't show.
portfolio.embedHint: Paste a YouTube, Vimeo, Loom or Figma link to embed it on your page.
portfolio.embedInvalid: Only YouTube, Vimeo, Loom and Figma links can be embedded.

testimonials.title: Client recommendations
testimonials.publishFirst: Publish your portfolio to collect recommendations.
testimonials.shareHint: Share this link with clients to collect recommendations:
testimonials.empty: No recommendations yet. Share your link to collect some.

cv.title: CV / Resume
cv.description: Generate an ATS-friendly CV from your profile, or upload an existing one to modernize it. Private — only you can see it. Download as PDF anytime.
cv.saveProfileFirst: Save your profile first to generate a CV.
cv.uploadHint: PDF or DOCX, max 5 MB. Your file is parsed in memory and never stored.
cv.errorUploadType: Only PDF and DOCX files are supported (old .doc is not).
cv.errorUploadEmpty: No text found — this looks like a scanned/image CV. Please upload a text-based PDF or DOCX.
cv.uploadRateLimited: Too many uploads this hour. Please try again later.
cv.empty: No CV yet. Generate one from your profile or upload an existing CV to get started.
cv.designHint: Pick a template and accent color. Visual templates use a colored column; the “Clean” template is single-column and the safest for strict ATS parsers.
cv.skillsHint: Comma-separated. Technical (hard) skills match ATS keywords best.
cv.bulletHint: One line each — action verb + what you did + a measurable result.
cv.atsHint: How well an Applicant Tracking System can read your CV. Updates as you edit.
cv.noIssues: No issues found — your CV looks ATS-ready.

issue.noEmail: Add a contact email — ATS needs it to reach you.
issue.noSkills: Add a dedicated skills section — ATS reads it for keywords.
issue.noExperience: Add at least one experience or project entry.
issue.fillerPhrases: Replace filler like “responsible for / helped” with action verbs.
issue.inconsistentDates: Use consistent dates that include a 4-digit year.

cv.issueLowKeywordCoverage: Cover more of the job's keywords (aim for 60%+).
cv.tailorHint: Rewrite and reorder your CV to match a saved job — only from what you already have.
cv.tailorNoJobs: Add jobs first to tailor your CV to them.
cv.tailorPastePlaceholder: Paste the full job description here — the CV will be tailored to it.
cv.tailorApplied: Tailored — review the changes and save.
cv.keywordHint: Paste a job description to see which of its key skills your CV already covers — and what's missing.
cv.keywordNone: No clear skill keywords found in that text. Try a fuller job description.
cv.titleMatchOk: Your CV includes the exact job title "{title}" — this is the single strongest ATS signal.
cv.titleMatchMissing: Add the exact job title "{title}" to your CV headline or a recent role — it's the single strongest ATS signal.
cv.keywordAllCovered: Great — your CV covers all detected keywords.
cv.keywordApprox: Approximate hint. For a precise rewrite, use “Tailor to a job” below.
cv.weakPhrase: Weak phrasing — lead with an action verb.
cv.atsVisualWarning: This template uses a colored column — great for humans, but strict ATS parsers read a single column best. Switch to “Clean” for applications parsed by ATS.
cv.summaryWords: {count, plural, one {# word} other {# words}} · aim for 50–100

accounts.title: My Platform Accounts
accounts.description: Enter your profile URL for each platform. In the future you'll be able to import your profile through these connections.

portfolioPublic.leadBody: Send a quick request and I'll get back to you by email.

admin.feedbackDesc: Product feedback submitted by users. Read-only; visible to admins in the ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist.

feedback.messagePlaceholder: Describe the bug, the idea, or anything on your mind…
feedback.errorSend: Couldn't send your feedback. Please try again.
feedback.rateLimited: You've sent a lot of feedback recently. Please try again later.

recommend.subtitle: Your recommendation helps them win better work. It appears after they approve it.

tour.clickHint: Click the highlighted area to continue

welcome.title: Welcome to Multifolio 👋
welcome.body: Let's take a 2-minute tour of the platform. We'll connect an account, build your profile with AI, browse jobs, and create your CV and portfolio. You can skip anytime.

connectNav.title: Connect a platform
connectNav.body: Start by opening Platforms. If you already freelance on LinkedIn, Upwork or Fiverr, you can import your public profile in one click.

connectCard.title: Pick a platform
connectCard.body: Open any platform to link your profile and pull in your headline, skills and portfolio. No account there yet? Skip this step.

adapt.title: Adapt with AI
adapt.body: One click rewrites your core profile into an optimized version for every platform. This is where Multifolio saves you hours.

profileNav.title: Your profile
profileNav.body: Now let's head to your Profile — the single source of truth every platform, CV and portfolio is generated from.

profileAi.title: AI profile suggestions
profileAi.body: Multifolio reads your connected public profiles and suggests a sharper headline, summary and skills. Apply them field by field — it's free.

jobsNav.title: Find work
jobsNav.body: Open Jobs to browse a live feed of remote roles matched to your skills.

jobsFeed.title: Save a feed
jobsFeed.body: Create a feed to get matching jobs delivered continuously, with an AI fit score and one-click proposal drafts for each.

cvNav.title: Build your CV
cvNav.body: Next up: your CV. Open the CV tab to generate an ATS-ready resume from your profile.

cvGenerate.title: Generate your CV
cvGenerate.body: Generate an ATS-friendly CV from your profile, then pick a template and tailor it to any job. You can also import an existing one.

portfolioNav.title: Your portfolio site
portfolioNav.body: Last stop: your portfolio. Open the Portfolio tab to spin up a public site.

portfolioGenerate.title: Generate your portfolio
portfolioGenerate.body: Turn your profile into a shareable portfolio site — pick a theme, curate your gallery, and publish it at your own /p/ link.

finish.title: You're all set! 🎉
finish.body: That's the whole platform. Complete the setup checklist to unlock bonus credits — and you can replay this tour anytime from Getting Started.

gettingStarted.title: Welcome to Multifolio
gettingStarted.subtitle: Complete these steps to get the most out of Multifolio.
gettingStarted.doneBody: You've completed every step — you're ready to win more freelance work.
gettingStarted.journeyHint: About 5 minutes, one step at a time.

verifyEmail.title: Verify your email
verifyEmail.desc: Confirm your address to secure your account.

extensionGuide.title: How to use the extension
extensionGuide.subtitle: Import your Upwork & Fiverr profile in seconds.
extensionGuide.demoCaption: Click this button that appears at the bottom-right of your profile page.

step1.title: Install the extension
step1.desc: Add the Multifolio extension from the Chrome Web Store.

step2.title: Sign in to Multifolio
step2.desc: Make sure you're logged into your account in the same browser.

step3.title: Open your profile & import
step3.desc: Open your Upwork/Fiverr profile page and click the “Import to Multifolio” button at the bottom-right.

step4.title: Review & save the draft
step4.desc: A wizard opens with your headline, summary and skills — review and save.

publicAnalysis.metaDescription: Paste your Upwork, Fiverr or LinkedIn profile and get an instant AI score with improvement tips. Free, no signup needed.
publicAnalysis.heroTitle: How strong is your freelancer profile?
publicAnalysis.heroSubtitle: Paste your profile link or text and get an instant AI score — including whether it would pass Upwork's approval bar.
publicAnalysis.urlHint: LinkedIn links work directly. For Upwork/Fiverr, paste your profile text instead.
publicAnalysis.textPlaceholder: Paste your headline, overview and skills here…

feed.searchEmpty: No jobs found for this search.
feed.starredEmptyHint: Star jobs from the Feed or Search to save them here.
feed.lowRelevanceWarn: This job doesn't look like a strong match for your profile. Analyze it anyway?

strength.noSignal: Add skills to your profile to see how well this feed fits you.
strength.suggestAdd: Suggested keywords — tap to narrow this feed to your strengths:

feed.proposalReadyHint: Auto-paste it on the Upwork apply page with the Multifolio extension, or copy it. You always review and submit yourself.

assistedApply.title: One-click apply
assistedApply.desc: On the platform's apply page, the Multifolio browser extension pastes your proposal into the cover-letter box in one click. You always review and submit — no auto-submit.

autoDraft.label: Draft proposals overnight
autoDraft.hint: When on, we draft proposals for new jobs matching this feed in the background, so they're ready in Applied when you return.
autoDraft.warning: Spends up to {count} × proposal credits per day, automatically. You always review and send yourself — never auto-submitted.

scam.title: This listing shows scam warning signs

items.offplatform: Directs you to apply or chat off-platform (Telegram/WhatsApp) — a common scam pattern.
items.upfront_payment: Asks for an upfront fee, deposit, or to buy a kit — legitimate jobs never charge you to work.
items.crypto_payment: Promises payment in cryptocurrency — a frequent red flag for fake jobs.
items.financial_info: Requests sensitive bank, card, or ID details — never share these to 'apply'.

scam.footnote: Automated heads-up, not a verdict — review the listing carefully before engaging.

feed.filterInactive: Inactive — current job sources don't provide this data yet.
feed.settingsProposalPromptPlaceholder: e.g. Always mention my 5 years of React experience, keep it under 150 words, end with a question…
feed.proposalPromptDefault: Write a concise, personalized proposal (120-180 words). Open by showing you understand the client's specific goal and reference a concrete detail from their posting. Add one proof-of-fit line: their need + a relevant result you delivered (with a number when possible) + confidence you can repeat it. Keep the tone warm and direct with no generic filler or buzzwords, and don't restate the whole job post. Close with a short, specific call to action or a question that invites a reply.
feed.settingsProposalPromptHint: Pre-filled with a recommended, research-based prompt — edit it to fit your style, or clear it to use the built-in default. Applied when generating proposals for jobs from this feed.

modal.title: Feed
modal.keywordsHint: Jobs must contain at least one of these keywords.
modal.excludeKeywordsHint: Jobs containing any of these keywords are hidden.
modal.excludeCountriesHint: Jobs from clients in these countries are hidden.
modal.minClientSpentHint: Current job sources don't report client spend yet — this filter activates when the data arrives; jobs without data stay visible.
modal.minScoreHint: Only applies to jobs you already scored; unscored jobs stay visible.
modal.notifyHint: Get a summary email when new matching jobs arrive (checked twice a day).

pseo.metaDescription: Adapt your profile, score matching jobs, and send tailored proposals — Multifolio helps {role}s win more work on {platform}. Free to start.
pseo.subtitle: One profile, adapted for {platform}. Score jobs before you spend time, and send proposals tailored to each client.
pseo.problemBody: Clients skim dozens of profiles and proposals — generic ones get ignored. Multifolio tailors your profile and every proposal to {platform} and to the job in front of you.
pseo.featureAdaptBody: Write your experience once. AI rewrites it in the right length, tone and keywords for {platform}.
pseo.featureScoreBody: Every job gets a fit score, so you spend effort only where you can actually win.
pseo.featureProposeBody: Generate a proposal tailored to each job with a built-in quality check — then apply in one click.
pseo.skillsBody: Make sure these stand out in your {platform} profile:
pseo.faqA1: Yes — start with 100 free credits, no card required. Pay only if you need more.
pseo.faqA2: Multifolio adapts your profile and proposals specifically for {platform}, plus LinkedIn, Upwork, Fiverr, Bionluk and Armut.
pseo.faqQ3: Do you apply to jobs for me?
pseo.faqA3: No auto-submit. Multifolio drafts a tailored proposal and fills it in — you always review and send it yourself.
pseo.finalTitle: Ready to win more {role} work on {platform}?
pseo.finalBody: Set up your profile once and start applying smarter — free to begin.
pseo.hubSubtitle: Pick your platform and role for a focused playbook — plus the free tools to put it into action.

import.title: Set up your profile in seconds
import.subtitle: Import from a platform you already use — we'll draft your Multifolio profile, you polish it.
import.reviewNote: AI drafts it, you edit every field — nothing is saved until you hit save.
import.urlHint: LinkedIn or GitHub works best. Other public profile URLs are supported too — or paste your text.
import.textPlaceholder: Open your profile page, select all, copy — and paste here.
import.fileHint: Tip: LinkedIn → your profile → "Save as PDF".
import.draftTitle: Here's your draft — review and save
import.extensionSource: Imported from your {platform} profile via the browser extension.

extensionPrivacy.metaDescription: Privacy policy for the Multifolio Profile Importer browser extension.
extensionPrivacy.title: Multifolio Profile Importer — Privacy Policy
extensionPrivacy.intro: The Multifolio Profile Importer is a browser extension that lets you import your own freelancer profile (Upwork, Fiverr, LinkedIn) into your Multifolio account with one click. This policy explains exactly what the extension does with data.
extensionPrivacy.collectedBody: The extension only acts when you click its import button on a profile page. At that moment it reads the visible text of the page you are viewing and the URLs of the profile photo and portfolio images. It does not read pages in the background, does not track your browsing, and does not collect passwords, messages, cookies of other sites, or any data from pages other than the one you explicitly import.
extensionPrivacy.useBody: The collected page text and image URLs are sent over HTTPS to your own Multifolio account, where an AI model extracts a profile draft (headline, summary, skills). You review and edit the draft before anything is saved to your profile. Nothing is imported or published without your explicit confirmation.
extensionPrivacy.sharingBody: We do not sell, rent, or share the data the extension processes with third parties. No advertising or analytics trackers are included in the extension.
extensionPrivacy.permissionsTitle: Why the extension needs its permissions
extensionPrivacy.permissionsBody: Host access to upwork.com, fiverr.com and linkedin.com is required so the import button can appear on your profile pages. Host access to multifolio-ecru.vercel.app is required to deliver the imported data to your account using your existing login session.
extensionPrivacy.retentionBody: Imported drafts are stored in your account until you review them (drafts older than one hour are ignored and overwritten by the next import). Saved profile data lives in your Multifolio account and can be edited or deleted by you at any time. Deleting your account removes all of it.

earnings.metaDescription: How much do you actually take home? Calculate your net freelance income after platform fees, transfer costs and taxes — Upwork, Fiverr, Bionluk.
earnings.heroTitle: How much do you actually take home?
earnings.heroSubtitle: Enter a gross amount and see what's left after platform commission, transfer fees and tax — instantly, no sign-up.
earnings.taxHint: Simplified estimate on the remaining amount — pick 0 if you handle tax separately (e.g. young entrepreneur exemption).
earnings.fxAuto: Live rate filled in automatically — you can edit it.
earnings.compareHint: Same gross, transfer and tax — only platform commission differs.
earnings.disclaimer: All rates are editable estimates and can change over time; this is not tax or financial advice. Check current platform and bank fees before deciding.
earnings.ctaTitle: Keep more of what you earn
earnings.ctaBody: Multifolio helps you win better-paying work across platforms — optimized profiles, matched jobs and proposals. Start free with 100 credits.

rate.metaTitle: Freelance Rate Calculator — What Should I Charge? | Multifolio
rate.metaDescription: Work out the hourly and day rate you need to hit your income goal — after platform fees, taxes and expenses. Free, instant, no sign-up.
rate.heroSubtitle: Set your target take-home income and we'll work backwards to the hourly and day rate you need — after platform commission, tax and expenses.
rate.expensesHint: Software, equipment, accountant, etc. — leave 0 if none.
rate.billableHint: Only hours you can actually invoice — admin, sales and breaks don't count.
rate.taxHint: Simplified estimate on income after platform fees — pick 0 if you handle tax separately.
rate.hoursNote: Based on {monthly} billable hours/month across {weeks} working weeks.
rate.infeasible: Add some billable hours and keep commission + tax under 100% to see a rate.
rate.disclaimer: All values are editable estimates and can change over time; this is not tax or financial advice. Check current platform and tax rates before deciding.
rate.ctaBody: Multifolio helps you win better-paying work across platforms — optimized profiles, matched jobs and proposals. Start free with 100 credits.

roi.metaTitle: Upwork Connects ROI Calculator — Are Your Bids Paying Off? | Multifolio
roi.metaDescription: See whether the Connects (or credits) you spend on proposals actually earn their money back. Free, instant, no sign-up.
roi.heroSubtitle: Enter how many proposals you send, your win rate and average project size — we'll show your return on every Connect you spend, and the win rate you need just to break even.
roi.connectsHint: How many Connects (or credits) each bid costs. Upwork bids are typically 4–16.
roi.costHint: Roughly $0.15 each on Upwork — edit if you buy in different bundles.
roi.winRateHint: Share of proposals that turn into paid work. New freelancers often see 2–8%.
roi.breakEven: You only need a {rate}% win rate to break even on Connects.
roi.profit: Every $1 of Connects returns about ${multiple} in project revenue.
roi.loss: At this win rate your Connects cost more than they earn — raise your win rate or project value, or spend fewer Connects per bid.
roi.infeasible: Enter proposals, Connects per proposal and an average project value to see your ROI.
roi.disclaimer: All values are editable estimates; Connect prices and win rates vary. This is guidance, not a guarantee.
roi.ctaBody: Multifolio scores jobs against your profile so you only spend Connects where you can actually win — plus proposals that convert. Start free with 100 credits.

compare.metaTitle: Freelance Platform Net Income Comparison — Multifolio
compare.metaDescription: One project, five platforms: see how much you take home on Upwork, Fiverr, Bionluk and direct clients after fees, transfer costs and tax.
compare.heroTitle: Same project — which platform pays you most?
compare.heroSubtitle: Enter one amount and compare your net take-home across platforms side by side, after commission, transfer fees and tax.
compare.armutNote: Armut isn't a percentage cut — it charges a per-lead fee (you pay for every quote you send, win or lose), so it can't be compared as a flat %. Factor in your win rate before bidding.

armutRoi.title: Armut: is this lead worth bidding on?
armutRoi.subtitle: Armut charges per quote whether you win or not. See the expected value and the win rate you need to break even.
armutRoi.disclaimer: Rough estimate, not advice. Use your realistic win rate and net project value (after Armut's win commission).

compare.disclaimer: Rates are editable estimates and change over time; this is not tax or financial advice. Transfer and tax are applied equally to every platform, so the ranking reflects platform commission.
compare.ctaBody: Multifolio turns one profile into optimized profiles and proposals for every platform — so you keep more per project. Start free with 100 credits.

trTax.metaDescription: Serving foreign clients from Turkey? Check if you qualify for the service-export income deduction (GVK 89/1-13) and the young entrepreneur exemption — and roughly how much tax base you can shield.
trTax.heroTitle: Are you leaving Turkish tax breaks on the table?
trTax.heroSubtitle: Freelancers serving foreign clients from Turkey can deduct most of that income. Answer a few questions to see which exemptions likely apply.

q.foreignClients: Do you serve clients based outside Turkey?
q.professionInScope: Is your work software, design, engineering, architecture, data processing or similar?
q.invoicesFromTurkey: Do you invoice from Turkey and bring the earnings into Turkey by the filing deadline?
q.youngFirstTime: Are you under 29 and registering as an income-tax payer for the first time?

trTax.incomeHint: Used only for a rough illustration of the deductible base — not the tax you owe.

hizmetIhraci.title: Service-export deduction (GVK 89/1-13)
hizmetIhraci.likely: Likely eligible — for {year} you may deduct {pct}% of this income from your tax base.
hizmetIhraci.no: Not eligible from your answers — all three conditions (foreign client, in-scope service, invoiced from Turkey) must hold.

gencGirisimci.title: Young entrepreneur exemption (GVK mük. 20)
gencGirisimci.likely: Likely eligible — for {year} up to {cap} of profit is exempt, for 3 years.
gencGirisimci.no: Not eligible from your answers (requires under 29 + first-time taxpayer).

est.title: Rough deductible base
est.shielded: ≈ {pct}% of your income shielded before income-tax brackets apply.

trTax.kdvNote: Bonus: services exported to clients abroad are VAT-exempt (0%, KDV code 302) — you charge no VAT and can reclaim input VAT.
trTax.bagkurNote: Note for 2026: the young-entrepreneur Bağ-Kur premium subsidy was removed — you now pay your own Bağ-Kur premium.
trTax.disclaimer: Informational only, not tax advice. Rates and caps change yearly and conditions are strict; the 2026 figures are recent — confirm with the official source and your accountant (mali müşavir) before relying on them.
trTax.ctaBody: Multifolio helps you win better-paying foreign clients across platforms — one profile, optimized everywhere. Start free with 100 credits.

legalPrivacy.metaDescription: How Multifolio collects, uses and protects your data.
legalPrivacy.title: Privacy Policy
legalPrivacy.intro: This policy explains what data Multifolio collects, why, and how we protect it. It applies to the Multifolio website and app.
legalPrivacy.draftNotice: This is a template for informational purposes and is not legal advice. Please have it reviewed by a legal professional before you start taking payments.
legalPrivacy.collectedBody:
• Account data: your email address and password (stored encrypted by our authentication provider).
• Profile data you enter: headline, summary, skills, and any profile text or files you import.
• CV data: if you upload a CV, the file is parsed in memory to extract its text and is not stored; we keep only the structured CV you choose to save, which stays private to your account.
• Data pulled from platforms you connect (e.g. LinkedIn, Bionluk): public profile information from the URL you provide.
• Usage data: the AI actions you run and the credits they consume.
We do not store payment card details.
legalPrivacy.useBody: We use your data to provide the service: generating platform-adapted profiles, proposals, job matches, portfolio pages and CVs, and to operate your account. AI generation is performed by our model provider solely to produce the output you request. Uploaded CV text is sent to our AI provider only to extract its content, then discarded.
legalPrivacy.sharingBody: We do not sell your data. We share it only with the service providers needed to run Multifolio: our hosting and database provider (Supabase), our AI provider (OpenAI) and our email provider (Resend). Content you choose to publish, such as a public portfolio page, becomes publicly accessible.
legalPrivacy.cookiesBody: We use essential cookies for authentication and to remember your language and theme preferences. We do not use advertising cookies.
legalPrivacy.securityBody: Access to your data is protected by row-level security, so each account can only reach its own records. AI keys and secrets are kept server-side only, and data is transmitted over encrypted connections.
legalPrivacy.rightsBody: You can view and edit your profile data at any time from your dashboard, and delete your account to remove your data. For requests, contact us at the address below.
legalPrivacy.contactBody: For privacy questions, contact us at yanlizcakaan@gmail.com.

legalTerms.title: Terms of Service
legalTerms.intro: These terms govern your use of Multifolio. By creating an account you agree to them.
legalTerms.draftNotice: This is a template for informational purposes and is not legal advice. Please have it reviewed by a legal professional before you start taking payments.
legalTerms.acceptanceBody: By accessing or using Multifolio, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the service.
legalTerms.serviceBody: Multifolio helps freelancers adapt their profile for multiple platforms, generate proposals, match with job listings and publish a portfolio page. The service is currently in beta and features may change.
legalTerms.accountBody: You are responsible for the accuracy of the information you provide and for keeping your login credentials secure. You must be legally able to enter into this agreement.
legalTerms.creditsBody: AI actions consume credits. New accounts start with free credits. Paid credit purchasing is not yet available; when it launches, applicable terms and pricing will be shown before purchase. Credits have no cash value and are non-refundable except where required by law.
legalTerms.acceptableBody: You agree not to misuse the service: no unlawful content, no attempts to breach security, no automated abuse, and no use that violates the rules of the third-party platforms you target.
legalTerms.ipBody: You retain ownership of the profile content you provide. AI-generated text is provided for your use. The Multifolio name, design and software remain our property.
legalTerms.disclaimerBody: The service is provided "as is" during beta. AI-generated output may contain errors — review it before use. We are not responsible for outcomes on third-party platforms, and calculators and scores are estimates, not guarantees.
legalTerms.changesBody: We may update these terms as the product evolves. Continued use after changes means you accept the updated terms.
legalTerms.contactBody: Questions about these terms? Contact yanlizcakaan@gmail.com.

legalKvkk.metaDescription: Information notice under Turkey's Personal Data Protection Law (KVKK).
legalKvkk.title: Data Protection (KVKK) Notice
legalKvkk.intro: This notice is provided under Article 10 of Turkey's Personal Data Protection Law No. 6698 ("KVKK") and explains how your personal data is processed.
legalKvkk.draftNotice: This is a template for informational purposes and is not legal advice. Please have it reviewed by a legal professional before you start taking payments.
legalKvkk.controllerBody: Multifolio is currently in beta and is not yet operated by a legal entity. As such, the data controller is the natural person operating the service. For requests and inquiries: yanlizcakaan@gmail.com. Once commercial operations begin, this text will be updated with the data controller's trade name, address, and tax/registration details.
legalKvkk.dataBody:
• Identity and contact: email address.
• Profile content you provide: headline, summary, skills, imported profile text or files.
• Public profile data from platforms you connect.
• Transaction and usage records: AI actions and credit usage.
legalKvkk.purposeBody: Your data is processed to create and operate your account, to deliver the requested AI outputs (profile adaptation, proposals, matching, portfolio), to provide support, and to meet our legal obligations.
legalKvkk.basisBody: Processing is based on the performance of a contract (KVKK Art. 5/2-c), our legitimate interests (Art. 5/2-f), compliance with legal obligations (Art. 5/2-ç), and, where required, your explicit consent.
legalKvkk.transferBody: To operate the service, data may be processed by our providers, including hosting and database (Supabase), AI processing (OpenAI) and email (Resend), which may be located abroad. Transfers are made in line with KVKK Articles 8 and 9.
legalKvkk.methodBody: Your data is collected electronically through the website, the app, the browser extension, and the connected platform URLs you provide.
legalKvkk.rightsBody: You have the right to learn whether your data is processed, to request information about it, to know the purpose, to request correction or deletion, to object, and to claim compensation for damages. To exercise these rights, contact the data controller above.
legalKvkk.contactBody: To exercise your rights or for questions, contact yanlizcakaan@gmail.com.

legalContact.metaDescription: Get in touch with the Multifolio team.
legalContact.title: Get in touch
legalContact.intro: Questions, feedback, or a legal request? We'd love to hear from you.
legalContact.note: We aim to reply within a few business days. This is a beta product, so your feedback directly shapes what we build next.
